title: US and Iran maintain diplomatic dialogue despite exchange of fire in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Donald Trump and Iranian officials have signaled a preference for continued diplomacy even after an exchange of missile and drone fire in the Strait of Hormuz. The clashes, which included the seizure of an oil tanker by Iran and reports of a Chinese tanker being attacked, have caused a spike in global oil prices and prompted ASEAN leaders to call for a joint response to the regional fallout.

title: Russia and Ukraine trade accusations over Victory Day ceasefire violations
summary: As Russia commemorates the 81st anniversary of the defeat of Nazi Germany, both Moscow and Kyiv have accused each other of breaching a holiday ceasefire. Ukraine reports over 140 strikes and 10 assaults by Russian forces, while Russian officials claim they are conducting retaliatory strikes and expressed indignation over Volodymyr Zelensky's diplomatic activities in Yerevan.

title: Donald Trump and Lula da Silva meet at White House to relink US-Brazil relations
summary: Donald Trump and Lula da Silva held a private meeting in the Oval Office aimed at pragmatically relinking the relationship between the United States and Brazil. While both leaders characterized the meeting as successful, observers noted lingering tensions regarding trade tariffs and regional security.

title: South African Constitutional Court orders Parliament to revisit Cyril Ramaphosa impeachment
summary: The Constitutional Court of South Africa has ruled that Parliament acted unlawfully when it blocked impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa. The decision has led to renewed calls for the president's resignation and warnings from political allies about the precedent of using judicial rulings for political vendettas.

title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship MV Hondius triggers international health response
summary: The World Health Organization and national health agencies are monitoring a hantavirus outbreak linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius. While cases have been confirmed in the UK and suspected in Spain, health officials have emphasized that the virus does not represent the start of a new pandemic, despite the US raising emergency alert levels.

title: Internal crisis hits Real Madrid following training ground altercation
summary: Real Madrid is facing a reputational crisis following a physical altercation between Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni. The club is reportedly searching for a 'topo' (mole) who leaked details of the fight and subsequent emergency meetings to the press ahead of the upcoming Clásico.

title: Tamil Nadu government formation proceeds with support for Vijay's TVK party
summary: Political shifts in Tamil Nadu see the Left and VCK parties extending support to Vijay's TVK for government formation. The development has caused friction within the Congress party and its existing alliance with the DMK.

title: US trade court strikes down Donald Trump's 10 percent universal tariffs
summary: A US trade court has ruled that the 10 percent global tariffs imposed by Donald Trump are illegal. The ruling comes as Trump issues an ultimatum to European trade partners to reach an agreement by July 4 and prepares for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

title: China sentences former defense ministers to death with reprieve for corruption
summary: Chinese state media announced that former defense ministers have been handed suspended death sentences following corruption investigations. The move is framed by the party as a demonstration that disloyalty and graft will not be tolerated within the high command of the military.

title: Pope Leo XIV marks first anniversary amid tensions with US administration
summary: As Pope Leo XIV completes his first year in office, analysts describe him as a moral antagonist to the global far-right. Tensions were noted during a meeting with Marco Rubio, reflecting a rift between the Vatican and the White House over the ongoing war in the Middle East.

title: Labour Party suffers significant losses in UK local elections as Nigel Farage gains
summary: Early results from British local elections show a major defeat for the Labour Party and a surge for Nigel Farage's political movement. Keir Starmer has stated he will not resign despite the results, which Farage described as a historic shift in British politics.

title: Bank of Mexico concludes interest rate cut cycle at 6.5 percent
summary: Banxico has ended its cycle of interest rate reductions, leaving the reference rate at 6.5 percent. The decision was made as inflation in Mexico showed signs of slowing to 4.45 percent in April, though some board members voted against the final cut.

title: K-pop group BTS begins successful concert residency in Mexico City
summary: BTS performed the first of three sold-out concerts at the Estadio GNP Seguros in Mexico City. The group's arrival included a visit to the National Palace to meet with Claudia Sheinbaum, while local authorities managed significant traffic and logistical challenges surrounding the event.

title: Mount Dukono eruption in Indonesia kills three hikers
summary: A volcanic eruption at Mount Dukono has resulted in the deaths of three hikers, including two Singaporeans. Indonesian rescue teams are searching for additional missing persons as the region remains on high alert for further volcanic activity.

title: Naturalist David Attenborough celebrates 100th birthday
summary: Global tributes have been paid to broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough as he reaches his 100th birthday. Coverage across multiple languages celebrates his role in creating the modern nature documentary genre and his lifelong commitment to wildlife conservation.
